response.justification: This candidate is an accomplished full-stack software engineer, but the role is a Principal Performance Architect focused on SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator hardware, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design. The resume shows no SoC/IP modeling, no deep-learning workload or accelerator optimization, and no GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture work. There is some adjacent low-level experience (C/C++ at National Instruments, embedded/robotic-arm work, high-performance low-latency services in D), but it falls far short of the hardware-architecture depth required. The educational paths (PhD/MS in a relevant field, or BS + 8 years) are only partially met: a BS in CS plus extensive experience exists, but the experience is not in the relevant hardware/accelerator domain.
strengths: Strong, broad software engineering background with high-performance/low-latency systems (D, async I/O, fibers)
Some low-level C/C++ and embedded experience (National Instruments, robotic arms)
Senior/staff-level seniority with proven delivery and engineering-discipline (TDD, CI/CD)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator, or GPU architecture experience
No deep neural network training/inference optimization or SIMD/MIMD background
Domain mismatch: web/mobile/full-stack profile vs. hardware performance-architecture role

key_factors.factor: No DL workload / GPU architecture optimization
